title: Full Stack AI Engineer - 8+ years in Python/Devops/Cloud
company_focus:
Information services for legal, tax, accounting, compliance,
government, and media professionals
description:
About the role:

As a Staff Software Engineer (Full Stack), you will be
hands-on in designing, building, and delivering innovative
AI-powered solutions, drive technical strategy and embed
within business units to reimagine processes and accelerate
TR's AI-first transformation.


Build & Design AI-Driven Systems

- Define technical standards and best practices for rapid
  prototyping and lead solution discovery

- Hands-on design and prototyping of complex, enterprise-scale
  AI-driven systems

- Personally build engaging front-ends using React, Next.js,
  TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS

- Directly develop robust back-ends with Python, FastAPI,
  Pydantic, and cloud infrastructure, including API design,
  data modeling, and AI service integration

Provide Senior Technical Leadership

- Contribute to technical design decisions across the stack,
  including architecture, API design, and system integration
  patterns

- Lead technical architecture decisions and translate high-
  level business requirements into comprehensive, scalable
  technical specifications

- Mentor other engineers on the team, fostering technical
  growth and career development

- Serve as a senior thought leader, driving technical
  discussions in executive forums and representing Forward
  Engineering at internal events

Lead Cross-Functional Collaboration

- Serve as a technical leader for full-stack projects through
  prioritization decisions, scoping out timelines, guiding
  the work of other engineers, and collaborating with cross-
  functional partners

- Embed directly with prioritized business units to develop
  deep domain expertise and build stakeholder relationships

- Partner with business leaders, functional SMEs, and product
  teams to co-create solutions and drive adoption

- Document solutions and architectural decisions for knowledge
  transfer and capability building

About You


You are a fit for the position if you have:

Required Skills and Experience


Core Engineering Experience

- Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, or
  equivalent experience; Master's degree preferred

- 8+ years of hands-on software engineering experience across
  the full stack (we welcome engineers who are stronger in
  front-end or back-end but have demonstrated capability in
  both)

- Strong leadership experience driving technical initiatives
  as an individual contributor

- Excellent strategic thinking, problem-solving skills, and
  ability to operate effectively in complex, fast-paced
  environments

- Outstanding communication and presentation skills with
  experience influencing senior stakeholders

Cloud & Infrastructure (Required)

- Deep, production-level experience with AWS or Azure - you
  should have designed, deployed, and operated systems on at
  least one of these platforms

- Experience with cloud orchestration and infrastructure tools
  (Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, CloudFormation, or
  similar)

- Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and modern DevOps practices
  (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, or similar)

AI & Machine Learning (Required)

- Proficiency in Python and practical experience working with
  AI systems, including:

- Basic understanding and hands-on experience with large
  language models (LLMs)

- Experience building or integrating AI agents or agentic
  workflows

- Familiarity with LLM orchestration frameworks (LangChain,
  LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel, or similar)

- Familiarity with AI/ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, or
  similar)

- Demonstrated expertise integrating AI models into production
  systems using APIs, data pipelines, and cloud-native
  architecture

- Basic understanding of vector databases and RAG (Retrieval-
  Augmented Generation) patterns

Data & APIs (Required)

- Experience designing and implementing RESTful APIs and/or
  GraphQL endpoints

- Hands-on experience with both SQL databases (PostgreSQL,
  MySQL, or similar) and NoSQL databases (MongoDB, DynamoDB,
  CosmosDB, or similar)

- Understanding of data modeling, database design, and
  optimization

Security & Best Practices

- Understanding of authentication and authorization patterns
  (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, or similar)

- Knowledge of security best practices including secrets
  management and secure API design



Bonus Points

Advanced AI/ML Experience

- 4+ years of experience leveraging advanced AI/ML frameworks
  in production systems

- Advanced knowledge of vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate,
  Chroma, pgvector), embeddings, semantic search, and
  information retrieval systems

- Experience with prompt engineering and management tools
  (LangSmith, PromptLayer, or similar)

- Experience with AI governance, model risk management, and
  regulatory compliance

Infrastructure & Scale

- Experience with message queues and event-driven
  architectures (Kafka, RabbitMQ, AWS SQS/SNS, Azure Service
  Bus, or similar)

- Experience with serverless architectures (AWS Lambda, Azure
  Functions)

Observability & Quality

- Production experience with APM and observability tools
  (Datadog, New Relic, Application Insights, or similar)

- Experience implementing comprehensive testing strategies
  (unit, integration, e2e) using modern testing frameworks

- Knowledge of distributed tracing and logging systems
  (OpenTelemetry, ELK Stack, CloudWatch)

#LI-NR1

What’s in it For You?

- Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working
  environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on
  the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a
  seamless experience that is digitally and physically
  connected.
- Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of
  supportive workplace policies designed to help manage
  personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring
  for family, giving back to the community, or finding time
  to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work
  arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8
  weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better
  work-life balance.
- Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of
  continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our
  talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-
  world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-
  first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to
  grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
- Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive
  benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-
  wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app,
  retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee
  incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical,
  and financial wellbeing.
- Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for
  inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance,
  and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our
  Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act
  Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
- Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our
  Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid
  volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get
  involved with pro-bono consulting projects and
  Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
- Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies
  globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth,
  and transparency. Together, with the professionals and
  institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law,
  turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the
  facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people
  all over the world.

About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together
the trusted content and technology that people and
organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve
professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance,
government, and media. Our products combine highly
specialized software and insights to empower professionals
with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make
informed decisions, and to help institutions in their
pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part
of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted
journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across
more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to
contribute and grow professionally in flexible work
environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy,
fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it
our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help
shape the industries that move society forward.

As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds,
perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on
our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek
talented, qualified employees in all our operations around
the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including
pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin,
religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital
status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other
protected classification under applicable law. Thomson
Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity
Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified
individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held
religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More
information on requesting an accommodation here.

Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job
postings here.

More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on
thomsonreuters.com.

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role requires hands-on work building and integrating AI
systems, including LLMs, AI agents, agentic workflows, and
RAG patterns. The engineer will directly develop AI-driven
systems and integrate AI models into production, which
qualifies as ai-first work rather than support.
